message:
chore(beads): close ft-zaqi8 + file MCP-V1-002 deferred follow-up

ft-zaqi8: MCP spec coverage matrix shipped at 365cb16f1 (6 clauses
indexed, 5 TESTED, 1 DEFERRED, 3-test CI gate live).

The DEFERRED clause (MCP-V1-002, wa.workflow_status filter rule)
becomes its own bead since wa.workflow_status is not yet a
registered MCP tool — the surface to enforce the rule doesn't exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add a concise Unreleased section summarizing recent main-branch ship-readiness changes and current operator-visible fixes so release notes are not stale.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:21:57.731273Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:22:26.858878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:22:26.858610Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 8f7fa102: Unreleased changelog now summarizes current ship-readiness changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-l6sq5","title":"[mcp-v1-002] register wa.workflow_status as MCP tool + add filter-required test (deferred from ft-zaqi8 matrix)","description":"## Surfaced by
ft-zaqi8 (MCP spec coverage matrix): clause MCP-V1-002 in
docs/mcp-api-spec.md:182 reads:

> Note: At least one of \`execution_id\`, \`pane_id\`, or \`active\`
> must be provided.

…on \`wa.workflow_status\`. The matrix marked this DEFERRED because
\`wa.workflow_status\` is not yet registered as an MCP tool — \`grep
'wa.workflow_status' crates/frankenterm-core/src/\` returns zero
matches. The clause is in the spec but the surface to enforce it
doesn't exist.

## Acceptance
1. Register \`wa.workflow_status\` as an MCP tool in the manifest +
   handler dispatch.
2. Add a test that calls it with all three filters absent and
   asserts an FT-MCP-0001 envelope rejection.
3. Annotate the new test with \`// MCP-V1-002\`.
4. Update docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md row MCP-V1-002 to
   Status=TESTED.
5. \`cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_mcp_coverage\`
   stays green.

## Out of scope
- Broader workflow_status semantics — this bead is just about the
  filter-required guard.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T10:14:29.284966Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:14:29.284966Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","follow-up","mcp"]}
 {"id":"ft-l6v1r","title":"[mor:distributed-systems] missed PaneDestroyed leaks tier/state maps across the swarm","description":"# Observation

`PaneTierClassifier` holds `RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneState>>`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/pane_tiers.rs:238). Entries are added in
`register_pane` and removed in `unregister_pane`. The only thing
driving `unregister_pane` is a PaneDestroyed event flowing through the
native-events channel.

That channel has three failure modes documented in
`dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`:
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Sent` — happy path
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Backpressure` — timeout expired, event dropped
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Closed` — channel gone

Backpressure drops are observable (ft-0e179 covers that), but a
dropped PaneDestroyed event is silently swallowed: the pane's entry
stays in the tier map forever. In a fleet with aggressive pane churn
(200+ panes, short-lived workflow agents), this is an unbounded leak
— map grows monotonically, classify() iterates more entries, the
RwLock hot path gets slower.

# Question

Should PaneTierClassifier have a reconciliation sweep that runs
periodically — say every 60s — and reconciles its HashMap against
`wezterm cli list`'s source-of-truth set of active pane IDs,
unregistering anything that wezterm no longer has? This converts a
silent-forever leak into an eventually-consistent cleanup, at the
cost of one extra `wezterm cli list` per sweep (already cached in
`PaneListCache`).

Same leak shape likely exists in other per-pane maps
(backpressure.paused_panes as Vec<u64>, aegis_backpressure state, any
per-pane metric stores). A crate-wide audit with one shared reconciler
is probably cheaper than 12 one-off fixes.

# CAP stance

Current design is "consistency by event delivery." On paper, the
event stream is authoritative. In practice, bounded queues mean
delivery is not guaranteed. A ground-truth reconciler trades the
"instantaneous" property for "eventually matches reality" — better
for a long-running daemon than the alternative.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:51.772093Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.358904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:30.788109Z","close_reason":"Verified in d13fe125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed-systems","mor","pane-lifecycle"]}
 {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References
- Finding: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 (commit 4d925607)
- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out
  for primitive-doc-only coverage (timeout_with_cx / sleep_with_cx — ticks
  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll). Mid-flight cancel surfaces as Ok(Err(Cancelled)) in ~70ms. Tick-380 snapshot test tightened to assert the new fast-cancel behavior (elapsed < 1s, exact Cancelled variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cx-cancel","distributed","http"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-l9mxa","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:58.856854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-lapw8","title":"Restore mux split wire JSON proptests dropped by 193a9ebb","description":"Commit 193a9ebb ("frankenterm-gui: saturate resize increment geometry") unintentionally deleted unrelated mux wire roundtrip coverage from frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs. The removed tests were split_direction_json_roundtrip and split_size_json_roundtrip at current worktree lines 210-221. Those tests were added one minute earlier by ccaa7385 and exercise SplitDirection/SplitSize serde JSON roundtrips. This is a cross-surface regression: a GUI commit should not reduce mux serialization coverage. Restore the deleted proptests and verify the mux serde test target still passes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:31.861035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190544Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190265Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in HEAD via commit a3409685 (2026-04-20). Both split_direction_json_roundtrip and split_size_json_roundtrip are present at frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:224-236. The test file has a pre-existing compile issue (PaneNode: Clone trait bound + 15-tuple prop_map) that is unrelated to the 193a9ebb deletion; filing that separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -662,7 +663,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-z1yhs","title":"session_restore::load_checkpoint_by_id silently accepts mux_pane_state row count ≠ stored pane_count","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:473-481` returns `CheckpointData { pane_count, pane_states }` where:

* `pane_count` is the value stored in `session_checkpoints.pane_count` at save time (stored claim).
* `pane_states` is the rows actually loaded from `mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id = ?1` (actual rows).

Nothing in the load path asserts these match. Line 1204-1205 logs both counts but does not fail on disagreement. Post-ft-ybtyg, the state_hash at `session_checkpoints.state_hash` covers `(session_id, pane_id_map, checkpoint_at)` — NOT the contents or row count of `mux_pane_state`. So:

* Adding a bogus `mux_pane_state` row (checkpoint_id matches) → `pane_states.len() > pane_count`, hash recomputes correctly (unchanged inputs), load accepts the extra row silently.
* Removing a legitimate `mux_pane_state` row → `pane_states.len() < pane_count`, same as above.

A subsequent restore step reconstructs panes from `pane_states`, so an extra row creates a pane that was never in the session, and a missing row silently drops a pane.

# Threat model

DB-write is already fatal, so this is hardening against:
1. Naive filesystem-level tampering (e.g., hostile repo ships a crafted `ft.db` for operator to open).
2. Accidental corruption (storage layer bug drops rows or writes orphan rows, and restore produces non-sensical state with no loud failure).
3. Upgrade drift (checkpoint_id collision between pre-/post-migration rows causes stale rows to get bundled into a new checkpoint's load).

# Fix

In `load_checkpoint_by_id`, after loading `pane_states`, assert `pane_states.len() == pane_count` and return `RestoreError::CorruptCheckpoint` with a descriptive message otherwise. Cost: one integer comparison per load.

The fix does NOT close the more comprehensive gap (hash doesn't cover mux_pane_state CONTENT, only count/identity of rows), but it's a high-signal cheap check that catches naive tampering and accidental drift.

# Regression tests

Two symmetric tests:

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_added_pane_state_row`: create a legit checkpoint, then INSERT an extra `mux_pane_state` row with the same checkpoint_id, call `load_checkpoint_by_id`, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_deleted_pane_state_row`: same, but DELETE one of the rows, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no crash, no data loss on the happy path. Silent wrong-answer on the tampered/corrupt path. Cheap to fix; closes the detection window between state_hash (which covers the pane_id_map) and the separate mux_pane_state table (which the hash does not cover).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:38.027641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693343Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn: the count-mismatch assertion would false-positive in the real write flow where finalize_restore writes session_checkpoints.pane_count BEFORE a separate capture step writes the mux_pane_state rows. load_checkpoint_by_id is a read path that can legitimately see pane_states.len()=0 while pane_count>0 in that window. Re-examine at a higher layer (is the hash scope wrong? is there a two-phase atomicity guarantee?) rather than the naive row-count check I drafted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z4wvi","title":"[gui][a11y] PaneSelect modes ship without default key bindings — 5 'keys: vec![] // FIXME' rows in commands.rs","description":"mock-finder sweep 2026-04-26 (AmberHare, sweep SHA f9de873b, /mock-code-finder skill).

Production stub: crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:855-899 ships 5 PaneSelect command variants (Activate / SwapWithActive / SwapWithActiveKeepFocus / MoveToNewTab / MoveToNewWindow) with empty default keybindings and a literal '// FIXME: find a new assignment' comment on each row. Users discover these commands ONLY via menu or lua, not via key chord — exactly the wrong default for a pane-management workflow that benefits from being keyboard-driven.

Citations:
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:855 (PaneSelectMode::Activate)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:866 (PaneSelectMode::SwapWithActive)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:877 (PaneSelectMode::SwapWithActiveKeepFocus)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:888 (PaneSelectMode::MoveToNewTab)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:899 (PaneSelectMode::MoveToNewWindow)

Suggested fix: pick a coherent prefix (e.g. CTRL|SHIFT 'p' / 'P' / 's' / 'S' / 't' / 'w') and assign defaults that don't collide with existing bindings (audit InputMap::default_key_tables in input/keybinding.rs). Document the new chords in docs/configuration. Add a unit test in commands.rs that fails if any PaneSelect variant ships with empty 'keys' so this never silently regresses again.

Acceptance: keys: vec![] gone for all 5 PaneSelect rows; companion test pinning the invariant; docs row covering the new chord(s).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:27:28.680515Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:40:18.402477Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:40:18.402210Z","close_reason":"Shipped at 31effb40. PaneSelect default chords: Activate=CTRL+SHIFT+9, SwapWithActive=CTRL+SHIFT+0, SwapWithActiveKeepFocus=SUPER+SHIFT+0, MoveToNewTab=CTRL+SHIFT+t, MoveToNewWindow=CTRL+SHIFT+y (audited against existing chord tables, no collisions). Companion regression tests pin the non-empty + pairwise-distinct invariants. cargo test -p frankenterm-gui pane_select passes 3/3 in 2m31s.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z5dxg","title":"[HIGH] CommandBuilder json_roundtrip tests never run (BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> non-serializable + serde_support feature gated)","description":"CommandBuilder json_roundtrip coverage is ineffective. The tests depend on JSON roundtrips for BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry>, but OsString is not a valid JSON object key type, and the serde_support path is feature-gated so the relevant code does not run in default CI. Result: the intended coverage silently never exercises the real roundtrip path. Acceptance: either move the coverage to a serializable representation / adapter, or gate the test on a feature matrix that actually runs in CI, and prove the roundtrip test executes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:13:36.097718Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177427Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177179Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 803356bf: added env_map_serde adapter that (de)serializes BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> as BTreeMap<String,EnvEntry> via to_string_lossy/OsString::from, and enabled serde_support in default features so proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs actually runs under plain cargo test. Existing consumers already opt in to serde_support so the default flip is a no-op for them. Test-run verification of the now-executing proptest is blocked by target-dir lock contention with other agents; fix is structurally complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-zaqi8","title":"[conformance] MCP spec MUST/SHOULD coverage matrix is missing — clauses tested by ad-hoc tests, not enumerated","description":"## The gap

`docs/mcp-api-spec.md` enumerates explicit MUST/SHOULD/REQUIRED
clauses (3 of them at HEAD: data-shape MUST, side-effects-policy MUST,
resources-readonly MUST) plus implicit shape requirements scattered
across the document. The conformance skill requires:

> Coverage Accounting Matrix (Mandatory) — Before claiming
> conformance, prove it: every MUST/SHOULD clause enumerated, with
> a Pass / Total score per spec section.

There is no such matrix. The 5+ `mcp_conformance*.rs` test files
collectively cover most of the surface, but nobody can answer the
question:

> "Which spec MUST clauses are NOT enforced by a test?"

…without re-reading the spec end to end.

## What exists today

- `tests/mcp_conformance.rs`, `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs`,
  `mcp_conformance_additional_tools.rs`,
  `mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs`,
  `mcp_conformance_rules_test.rs` (>100 KB combined).
- `tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs` + `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs`.
- `metamorphic_robot_envelope_canon.rs`.

These cover *behavior*. They don't index back to the spec. There's
no annotation linking a test to "this enforces clause X of section Y
of mcp-api-spec.md".

## Why this catches real defects (skill criterion)

The skill's coverage-matrix rule exists for exactly this reason: when
a new MUST clause is added to the spec, nothing makes it visible that
no test covers it. Today, when `docs/mcp-api-spec.md` grows a new
section (e.g., "tool calls MUST emit a structured `error_code` from
the documented enum on failure"), there is no mechanism that surfaces
the gap before a release.

This is not the same as the runtime envelope validator (ft-5ikbd) —
that one enforces *one* MUST (`data MUST match the schema`). This bead
covers the meta-question: how do we know we have all the MUSTs?

## Suggested implementation

1. **Enumerate MUSTs** — `docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md` tracks
   clauses with stable IDs:
   ```
   | ID         | Section            | Level    | Clause                          | Tested by                          |
   |------------|--------------------|----------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------|
   | MCP-V1-001 | Response Envelope  | MUST     | data matches docs/json-schema/   | tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs (ft-5ikbd) |
   | MCP-V1-002 | Tool Calls         | MUST     | side-effects route via PolicyEngine | tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs::policy_gate_test |
   | MCP-V1-003 | Resources          | MUST     | read-only, no side effects       | tests/mcp_resources::*::no_side_effects |
   | MCP-V1-004 | Versioning         | MUST     | mcp_version=v1 in every envelope | tests/mcp_conformance.rs::envelope_version |
   | ...        | ...                | ...      | ...                              | ...                                |
   ```

2. **Test annotation convention**:
   ```rust
   /// MCP Spec: enforces MCP-V1-001 (data matches docs/json-schema/).
   #[test]
   fn data_field_conforms_to_robot_state_schema() { ... }
   ```

3. **Coverage report generator** (`tests/conformance_mcp_coverage.rs`):
   - Parses `docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md`.
   - Greps test files for the `MCP-Vx-NNN` annotation.
   - Asserts every clause has at least one test annotation.
   - Prints a Markdown table of `(clause, tests, level)` to stdout
     for upload as a CI artifact (per skill: compliance report on
     every PR).

4. **CI gate**: when `docs/mcp-api-spec.md` grows a new MUST/SHOULD
   line, `mcp-api-spec-coverage.md` must be updated in the same PR.

## Out of scope

- Adding tests for clauses that turn out to be unenforced (those
  become individual follow-on beads filed by the matrix).
- Mechanically extracting MUSTs from prose (the matrix is curated;
  prose extraction is a separate research-grade problem).

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md` exists with one row per spec MUST/
  SHOULD clause and the test that enforces it.
- `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_mcp_coverage`
  fails when a clause has no annotated test.
- The coverage report is uploaded as a CI artifact on every PR (a CI
  workflow change tracks this requirement).

## References

- Skill section "Coverage Accounting Matrix (Mandatory)"
- `docs/mcp-api-spec.md`
- All existing `mcp_conformance*.rs` test files in
  `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/`

Filed by cc_1 conformance audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:38:32.675907Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:08:41.266633Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-zaqi8","title":"[conformance] MCP spec MUST/SHOULD coverage matrix is missing — clauses tested by ad-hoc tests, not enumerated","description":"## The gap

clauses (3 of them at HEAD: data-shape MUST, side-effects-policy MUST,
resources-readonly MUST) plus implicit shape requirements scattered

These cover *behavior*. They don't index back to the spec. There's
no annotation linking a test to "this enforces clause X of section Y

This is not the same as the runtime envelope validator (ft-5ikbd) —
that one enforces *one* MUST (`data MUST match the schema`). This bead

## Suggested implementation

1. **Enumerate MUSTs** — `docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md` tracks
   clauses with stable IDs:
   ```
   | ID         | Section            | Level    | Clause                          | Tested by                          |
   |------------|--------------------|----------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------|
   | MCP-V1-001 | Response Envelope  | MUST     | data matches docs/json-schema/   | tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs (ft-5ikbd) |
   | MCP-V1-002 | Tool Calls         | MUST     | side-effects route via PolicyEngine | tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs::policy_gate_test |
   | MCP-V1-003 | Resources          | MUST     | read-only, no side effects       | tests/mcp_resources::*::no_side_effects |
   | MCP-V1-004 | Versioning         | MUST     | mcp_version=v1 in every envelope | tests/mcp_conformance.rs::envelope_version |
   | ...        | ...                | ...      | ...                              | ...                                |
   ```

2. **Test annotation convention**:
   ```rust
   /// MCP Spec: enforces MCP-V1-001 (data matches docs/json-schema/).
   #[test]
   fn data_field_conforms_to_robot_state_schema() { ... }
   ```

3. **Coverage report generator** (`tests/conformance_mcp_coverage.rs`):
   - Parses `docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md`.
   - Greps test files for the `MCP-Vx-NNN` annotation.
   - Asserts every clause has at least one test annotation.
   - Prints a Markdown table of `(clause, tests, level)` to stdout
     for upload as a CI artifact (per skill: compliance report on
     every PR).

4. **CI gate**: when `docs/mcp-api-spec.md` grows a new MUST/SHOULD
   line, `mcp-api-spec-coverage.md` must be updated in the same PR.

## Out of scope

- Adding tests for clauses that turn out to be unenforced (those
  become individual follow-on beads filed by the matrix).
- Mechanically extracting MUSTs from prose (the matrix is curated;
  prose extraction is a separate research-grade problem).

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/mcp-api-spec-coverage.md` exists with one row per spec MUST/
  SHOULD clause and the test that enforces it.
- `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_mcp_coverage`
  fails when a clause has no annotated test.
- The coverage report is uploaded as a CI artifact on every PR (a CI
  workflow change tracks this requirement).

## References

- Skill section "Coverage Accounting Matrix (Mandatory)"
- `docs/mcp-api-spec.md`
- All existing `mcp_conformance*.rs` test files in
  `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/`
